dhcp-basic.title: DHCP Host Configuration
starterPrompts: What is happening in this DHCP exchange?
Which hosts are involved, and what stage of host configuration is this?
Suggest a display filter that focuses on the DHCP traffic.
dhcp-basic.query: Identify the main protocol in this capture and summarize the host-configuration activity taking place.

dns-lookups.title: DNS Lookups
starterPrompts: What domains are being looked up here?
Is this mostly queries, responses, or both?
What filter would isolate the DNS traffic?
dns-lookups.query: What protocol is shown here and what kind of activity is happening?

http-sample.title: HTTP Requests
starterPrompts: What HTTP activity is in this capture?
Are there multiple HTTP requests or just one?
Which endpoints appear to be client and server?
http-sample.query: What application-layer protocol is shown and what kind of user activity does it appear to capture?

sip-rtp-sample.title: SIP and RTP Sample
starterPrompts: Does this capture contain VoIP signaling and media?
Which protocol handles signaling, and which carries the audio?
What filter would isolate the call setup messages?
sip-rtp-sample.query: What two protocols dominate this VoIP capture: the signaling protocol and the media protocol?

sip-g711-call.title: SIP RTP G.711 Call
starterPrompts: What evidence suggests this is a phone call?
Which signaling protocol is used?
Suggest a filter for only the RTP packets.
sip-g711-call.query: Does this capture show VoIP traffic, and which signaling protocol is used?

arp-storm.title: ARP Storm
starterPrompts: What anomaly is visible in this ARP capture?
Is there evidence of repeated ARP requests?
How would you filter to focus on the noisy traffic?
arp-storm.query: What anomaly is visible in this capture?

slammer-worm.title: Slammer Worm Traffic
starterPrompts: What transport protocol and port stand out here?
Does this resemble worm traffic or scanning behavior?
What filter would isolate the suspicious packets?
slammer-worm.query: What transport protocol and destination port stand out in this capture, and does it resemble worm traffic?

dns-remoteshell.title: DNS Remote Shell Anomaly
starterPrompts: Does this look like normal DNS or suspicious payload behavior?
What signs point to tunneling or command traffic over DNS?
Which filter would narrow this to the suspicious DNS packets?
dns-remoteshell.query: Does this capture look like normal DNS, or does it show suspicious payload behavior over DNS?

teardrop-attack.title: Teardrop Fragmentation Attack
starterPrompts: Is there evidence of overlapping IP fragments here?
What attack pattern is visible in these packets?
What filter would isolate the malformed fragments?
teardrop-attack.query: What attack pattern is present in these packets?
